name: Scalekit MCP vs CLI benchmark
summary: Scalekit ran 75 paired trials (Claude Sonnet 4) on five read-only GitHub repository tasks comparing GitHub Copilot MCP (43 tools) against gh CLI and CLI+Skills. MCP consumed far more tokens per task; remote MCP runs failed 28% of the time with ConnectTimeout while CLI completed all 25 runs. When connected, both modalities achieved task-level success on the measured operations.
methodology_notes: Five GitHub read-only tasks; modalities include MCP, gh CLI, and CLI+Skills (~800-token skill file). Open harness: scalekit-inc/mcp-vs-cli-benchmark. Published Mar 2026. Statistical significance claimed for suite-level token differences.

primary_sources.title: MCP vs CLI: Benchmarking AI Agent Cost & Reliability

implications_for_gax: Validates GAX core bet: lazy discovery (doc/search) is required to avoid 40k+ schema tax per session. gaxd sidecar + adapters address MCP reliability via local broker rather than model-facing remote tool list. Cap-per-invoke preserves MCP-class governance without shipping full schemas to the model.
corrections_to_repo_docs: research/01: use 4x-32x and 28% failure with ConnectTimeout citation; distinguish task success when connected vs run completion.
